opp.name: Drees Homes_Sourcing&SRPM_Opp_June'26
opp.next_step: Grace has sent slides, case studies, and Showpad videos to Michael for internal review. Michael will share the materials with IT, VPs, and other stakeholders to assess interest. Zycus should follow up after the review to schedule a focused discussion on iSource, Supplier Risk and Performance Management, and potential next steps for a live demo.
opp.ais_why:
• Drees Homes' discovery surfaced interest in 'iSource, Supplier Risk and Performance Management, Spend Analysis, Merlin Intake, and Autonomous Negotiation Agent' ‚Äî the ANA agent is explicitly on the interest list.
• The full agentic suite (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin, Merlin Intake) is scoped, consistent with that stated interest.
• Interest in the autonomous agent sits among several modules and core pains are decentralized purchasing and pricing visibility, so appetite is curious-leaning rather than hungry.

sources: Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c all null
Next Step 2026-06-21: materials sent for internal review, no business-case discussion recorded
Zero Avoma calls discovered for account Drees Homes in 365-day window
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI articulated. Salesforce free-text fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) returned null. Zero buyer calls discovered, so no transcript evidence of pain quantification. The Next Step notes Michael will share materials with IT and VPs to 'assess interest' but does not name a cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings target. Without a quantified value case, this deal has no anchor for pricing or urgency. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with Michael and surface the business problem in measurable terms (spend under management, number of suppliers, contract leakage, cycle time for RFP/sourcing events, manual effort in risk monitoring) so a defensible ROI can be built.

sources: Salesforce Contact Role: Michael Goedde, MBA, Decision Maker, Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager
Next Step 2026-06-21: Michael reviewing materials and sharing with IT/VPs (responsive but no advocacy evidence)
Last contact 2026-06-09 per LastActivityDate; zero buyer calls to assess influence or coaching behavior
champion.narrative: Michael Goedde is the sole contact and is assigned Decision Maker role, but champion strength is unproven. He is Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager per contact role. Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-09 (19 days ago); Next Step 2026-06-21 indicates Grace sent materials and Michael committed to internal review and sharing with IT/VPs, which is a positive signal of engagement. However, there is no evidence of advocacy (no introduction to VPs or IT, no competitor push-back on our behalf, no internal selling visible), no multi-threading (Michael is the only contact), and no buyer calls to assess his influence or access to the Economic Buyer. Champion status is 'developing' at best: he is engaged and responsive, but we have not validated his ability to drive the deal or his willingness to coach us on internal politics. The move is to deepen the relationship on the next call: ask Michael what he needs to build internal consensus, offer to co-present to IT or VPs, and assess whether he will advocate for Zycus or is running a neutral evaluation.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: Coupa;GEP Worldwide;JAGGAER;SAP Ariba
Zero Avoma calls; no competitive validation or buyer mention of evaluating other vendors
Next Step and task history: no mention of competitor demos, RFP responses, or competitive positioning
competition.narrative: Four competitors logged in Salesforce Competitors__c field (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba) but none validated with the buyer. Zero buyer calls and zero tasks/next-step mentions of competitive discussions mean this is a theoretical list, likely pre-filled by the rep based on market knowledge, not confirmed by the relevant stakeholder risk is that the real competition is different (a niche player, an incumbent, or a build-vs-buy decision), or that Drees has not yet engaged competitors and will run a formal RFP that brings all four into play. Without knowing which competitors Drees is actively evaluating, we cannot tailor differentiation. The move is to ask Michael on the discovery call: are you looking at other vendors, have you engaged any of them, and what are the key differences you are evaluating between platforms. If competition is real, the wedge is Merlin AI-powered sourcing and supplier risk automation (negotiation agent, risk scoring) vs Coupa's bolt-on AI and Ariba's legacy UI.

sources: Salesforce Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c all null
Product scope iSource;iSupplier;iRisk implies sourcing and risk pain, but buyer has not stated it
Zero Avoma calls; no pain articulation on record
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain not articulated. Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c all null. How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c is null, so current-state pain is undocumented. Zero buyer calls means no transcript of the buyer describing what is broken, costly, or risky in their current sourcing and supplier risk process. Product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iRisk) implies sourcing and risk pain, but that is inferred, not confirmed. Without a named pain owner and a quoted pain statement, there is no urgency anchor. The move is to run a discovery call and ask: what is the specific problem Drees is trying to solve (manual sourcing events, lack of supplier risk visibility, contract leakage, compliance exposure), who owns that problem day-to-day, and what is the cost of not solving it this year.

sources: Salesforce Current_Contract_Expiration__c null; no contracting or legal fields populated
Next Step 2026-06-21: no mention of MSA, SOW, InfoSec, or procurement channel
Zero Avoma calls; no contracting discussion on record
paper_process.narrative: Paper process unknown. No mention of MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or SI/partner involvement in any Salesforce field or Next Step. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so no incumbent-contract forcing function. Zero buyer calls means no discussion of contracting mechanics or IT security requirements. For a $120k SaaS deal with ERP integration (JD Edwards mentioned in prior sweep memory), expect InfoSec review and IT approval of API/integration scope, but neither is documented. The move is to surface this on the discovery call: what is Drees's standard procurement and contracting process for SaaS platforms, does IT or InfoSec need to review before contracting, and is there a preferred SI or implementation partner.

sources: Salesforce X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c null
Contact roles: one Decision Maker (Michael Goedde, Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager), zero Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor roles
Next Step 2026-06-21: Michael to share with 'VPs' but no VP named or budget-approval process clarified
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not identified. No contact role of Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor. Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c all returned null. Next Step states Michael will share materials with 'VPs' but does not name them or clarify budget authority. Michael Goedde's title is Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager, suggesting he is an operational stakeholder, not the commercial authority for a $120k enterprise SaaS deal. Zero buyer calls means no transcript evidence of who controls pricing or contract approval. The risk is that Michael drives evaluation but cannot approve budget, and the deal stalls at VP or CFO gate. The move is to ask Michael directly on the next call: who approves the budget for a sourcing and risk platform investment of this size, and can we brief them on the business case before the formal demo.

sources: Next Step 2026-06-21: Michael to review materials, share with IT and VPs, then schedule demo
Salesforce What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c null
Zero buyer calls; process is inferred from Next Step, not confirmed with buyer
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is partially known from Next Step but not validated with the buyer. Next Step 2026-06-21 states: Michael reviews materials internally, shares with IT and VPs to assess interest, then Zycus follows up to schedule a demo. This implies a two-stage informal process (internal review, then demo), but it does not clarify: who are the VPs, what is IT's role (advisory vs veto), is this leading to a formal RFP or sole-source evaluation, and who makes the final decision. Salesforce field What_is_the_decision_process__c is null. Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c is null, so approval chain is unknown. No buyer calls to read the process from participation sequence. The move is to confirm the process on the next call: after the demo, what are the formal gates (RFI, RFP, vendor shortlist, proof of concept, contract review), and who is involved at each stage.

sources: Salesforce Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c null
Products__c: iSource;iSupplier;iRisk (product scope set, but evaluation criteria unknown)
Zero Avoma calls; no use-case or RFP discussion on record
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria not documented. Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c all null. No RFI, RFP, or structured evaluation artifact referenced in Next Step or tasks. Zero buyer calls means no transcript of use-case priorities, must-have vs nice-to-have features, or scoring rubric. The only signal is product scope (iSource, iSupplier, iRisk) and a mention of JD Edwards and Microsoft Powerapps integration in prior sweep memory, but technical requirements have not been validated with the relevant stakeholder criteria, we cannot position differentiation or prepare a demo agenda that maps to their scorecard. The move is to run a discovery call and extract: what are the top 3–5 capabilities Drees is scoring vendors on, what is weighted highest (cost savings vs risk visibility vs supplier collaboration), and are there mandatory integration or compliance requirements.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

items.name: Michael Goedde, MBA
items.risk: Single point of contact; no multi-threading. If Michael becomes unavailable or IT/VP review does not generate interest, deal stalls. Champion strength unproven.
items.title: Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager

items.detail: Economic Buyer not identified; Michael requires VP and IT approval but no C-level sponsor or CFO engaged. Risk of stalling at VP level if budget authority is unclear or if IT/VP review does not generate executive sponsorship.

Single-threaded relationship with Michael Goedde; no multi-threading to IT, VPs, or other regional/corporate procurement stakeholders. Risk of deal stalling or losing momentum if Michael becomes unavailable or if internal review does not generate additional stakeholders.

JD Edwards and Microsoft Powerapps integration is a stated requirement but technical feasibility, API availability, and IT approval process not yet validated. Risk of IT blocking or delaying if integration is complex or requires custom development.

Competitive landscape is theoretical (four competitors logged but none validated with buyer). Risk of surprise competitor surfacing during IT/VP review or formal RFP process, especially if Drees decides to run a competitive evaluation.

items.action: Run discovery call with Michael Goedde to extract business case (quantified pain: cost, headcount, cycle time, supplier risk exposure), map buying committee (name the VPs and IT stakeholders he is sharing materials with), confirm decision process (RFI/RFP or sole-source), and validate competitive landscape (are other vendors engaged). Use this call to establish MEDDPICC foundation before scheduling demo.
items.trigger: Zero buyer calls discovered; no quantified pain, Economic Buyer, or decision criteria documented
items.expected_effect: Establishes business case, maps buying committee, validates competition, and qualifies whether this is a real opportunity or early-stage exploration. Moves deal toward Formal Evaluation gate.

items.action: Ask Michael on the discovery call to name the VPs and IT stakeholders reviewing the materials, and request a brief intro call with at least one VP and the IT lead to understand their priorities and concerns before the formal demo. Multi-threading to IT is critical to validate JD Edwards and Powerapps integration feasibility early.
items.trigger: Single contact (Michael Goedde); IT and VPs mentioned in Next Step 2026-06-21 but not engaged
items.expected_effect: Reduces key-person risk, validates technical integration requirements with IT, and builds relationships with VP-level stakeholders who likely influence or control budget approval.

items.action: On the discovery call, ask Michael who approves the budget for a sourcing and risk platform investment of this size ($120k). If it is a VP or CFO, request that Michael set up a brief executive briefing so Zycus can present the business case and ROI directly to the Economic Buyer before the demo.
items.trigger: Economic Buyer not identified; Michael's title (Regional Purchasing & Operations Manager) suggests operational role, not budget authority
items.expected_effect: Maps Economic Buyer, validates budget authority, and creates executive-level sponsorship early, reducing risk of stalling at VP gate later in the cycle.

items.action: Confirm with Michael on the discovery call whether Drees is evaluating other vendors (Coupa, GEP, JAGGAER, Ariba are logged competitors) or running a sole-source evaluation with the relevant stakeholder competitors are active, run a competitive positioning workshop internally to prepare differentiation messaging (Merlin AI vs Coupa bolt-on AI, unified platform vs GEP modular, modern UI vs Ariba legacy) before the demo.
items.trigger: Four competitors logged in Salesforce but none validated with buyer; no competitive discussion on calls or in tasks
items.expected_effect: Clarifies competitive landscape, allows tailored demo positioning, and reduces risk of surprise competitor surfacing during IT/VP review or RFP.

items.action: After the discovery call, prepare a one-page business-case summary for Michael to share with IT and VPs: quantified pain (from discovery call), Zycus solution fit (iSource sourcing automation, iRisk supplier risk visibility, JD Edwards integration), ROI estimate, and next steps. This gives Michael a tool to sell internally and accelerates IT/VP engagement.

Michael committed to internal review, which is a positive engagement signal, but no buyer call, no multi-threading, and no Economic Buyer mapped. Confidence is medium: deal is live and responsive, but thin on validation and breadth.

north_star_verdict.math: 474 days to close from 2027-10-15. Created 2026-06-12, 16 days old. Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-09 (19 days ago). No submission-date series fields populated (Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c all null per Salesforce read), so time-in-stage computed from last stage-change or creation. Deal is in early Qualified stage; typical enterprise motion allows weeks between formal milestones, so 19-day gap is normal for this phase. No forward slip detected (CloseDate has not changed per field history). Pace required: move to Formal Evaluation within 90–120 days to stay on track for Oct 2027 close.
evidence: Pipeline forecast is appropriate for early Qualified stage with no buyer calls, no Economic Buyer mapped, and materials still under internal review.

Products in scope: iSource, iSupplier, iRisk per Salesforce Products__c field.
north_star_verdict.headline: Qualified deal with 19 days of radio silence and zero buyer calls completed. Single-threaded to one contact; Economic Buyer, pain metrics, and competitive landscape unmapped. Needs champion activation and discovery execution to progress toward Formal Evaluation.

flags: Secure introductions to IT and VP stakeholders during the follow-up call to multi-thread beyond Michael Goedde and validate technical integration requirements (JD Edwards, Powerapps, Teams) and security/compliance process. Michael is a developing champion but does not control budget; mapping IT and VPs is critical to advance from Qualified to Shortlisted and identify the Economic Buyer.

Build a quantified business case (ROI, cost savings target, cycle time reduction) tied to iSource RFP efficiency and Supplier Risk cost transparency to justify budget and engage C-level sponsors. Michael articulated clear pain but no quantified impact; a data-driven business case will accelerate VP and EB engagement and differentiate Zycus from competitors.

Validate the competitive landscape and position Zycus' AI differentiation (Merlin, Anna negotiation agent, proactive external risk alerts) early before any formal RFP process. Four competitors are logged (GEP, Coupa, Ariba, JAGGAER) but none validated with the buyer; understanding whether Drees will run a competitive evaluation or prefers sole-source will shape the strategy and allow proactive competitive positioning.
No buyer calls discovered; zero transcript evidence of pain, criteria, or competitive validation. Run discovery call to establish business case and MEDDPICC foundation.

Salesforce custom fields Q2 (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c, Qualified_Submission_Date__c, Formal_Eval_Submission_Date__c, Shortlisted_Submission_Date__c, Current_Contract_Expiration__c) all returned null; no AI-fit signal or stage-date series for time-in-stage math.

Salesforce custom fields Q3 (MEDDPICC and competition synthesis fields: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Business_Requirements__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c) all null or not present in org. MEDDPICC read is based solely on contact roles and Next Step, not rich free-text fields.
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Zycus. If')
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: 365 days trailing for field history; 90 days for tasks/events
